title: limnanthes floccosa var. pumila
intro: Limnanthes floccosa var. pumila, also known as the dwarf meadow foam or creeping meadow foam, is a perennial herb that belongs to the family Limnanthaceae. It is native to California and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its high content of limnanthol and other bioactive compounds. The herbal preparations of Limnanthes floccosa var. pumila include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
